source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A village of 12 houses has four straight streets and four circular streets. The map shows 11 houses. In each straight street there are three houses and in each circular street there are also three houses. Where should the 12th house be placed on this map?
<image1>

image_dependency: Question explicitly refers to a map and asks where the 12th house should be placed on this map
Solving depends on spatial/visual street-house arrangement, not text alone
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and image_manifest shows a valid image exists
multi_step: Question combines multiple constraints: 12 houses total, four straight streets, four circular streets, and three houses on each type of street
It requires integrating the textual counting constraints with the visual map that currently shows 11 houses
Answer must be selected by checking which candidate location satisfies all street-count conditions
choices contains 5 candidate placements, consistent with a constrained elimination task
verifiability: Answer is a clear discrete label: answer_text='On D'
choices provide a finite set of candidate locations (A-E), making correctness easy to check
The counting conditions in the prompt are explicit and rule-based, so a verifier can test each candidate against the map
asset_registry_record shows question and answer are present and registry_passed=true
